name: CI/CD Pipeline Builder — Stack Detection + Pipeline Gen
description: Detect project stack and generate a complete CI/CD pipeline. Covers build, test, lint, security scan, and deploy stages for GitHub Actions.
template:
## CI/CD Pipeline Builder

### Stack Detection
Inspect project files to determine pipeline stages:
```bash
# Node.js
[[ -f package.json ]] && echo "node"
# Python
[[ -f requirements.txt || -f pyproject.toml ]] && echo "python"
# Docker
[[ -f Dockerfile ]] && echo "docker"
# Has tests
[[ -d __tests__ || -d tests || -f jest.config.* ]] && echo "tests"
```

### Node.js Pipeline Template
```yaml
name: CI
on:
  push: { branches: [main, develop] }
  pull_request: { branches: [main] }

jobs:
  ci:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4
      - uses: actions/setup-node@v4
        with: { node-version: '20', cache: 'pnpm' }
      - run: pnpm install --frozen-lockfile
      - run: pnpm run lint
      - run: pnpm run typecheck
      - run: pnpm test -- --coverage
      - name: Security scan
        uses: semgrep/semgrep-action@v1
        with: { config: 'p/javascript p/owasp-top-ten' }
      - name: Upload coverage
        uses: codecov/codecov-action@v4

  deploy:
    needs: ci
    if: github.ref == 'refs/heads/main'
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4
      - run: pnpm install --frozen-lockfile
      - run: pnpm build
      # Add your deploy step here
```

### Required Secrets (add to repo settings)
```
NODE_AUTH_TOKEN   # npm publish
CODECOV_TOKEN     # coverage upload
DEPLOY_KEY        # deploy target
```

### Branch Protection Rules
Protect `main`:
- Require status checks: ci
- Require up-to-date branches
- Dismiss stale reviews
- Block force push

### Checklist
- [ ] Pipeline runs on PR and push to main
- [ ] Lint + typecheck before tests
- [ ] Tests run with coverage report
- [ ] Security scan in CI
- [ ] Deploy only triggers on main after all checks pass
- [ ] Secrets stored in repo settings, not in workflow file
- [ ] Branch protection rules configured

name: GitHub Ops — PR/Issue Management
description: Automate PR creation, issue management, and GitHub workflow operations using gh CLI. Covers labels, milestones, reviewers, and PR descriptions.
template:
## GitHub Ops with gh CLI

### Create PR with full description
```bash
gh pr create \
  --title "feat: add user authentication" \
  --body "$(cat <<'EOF'
## Summary
- Implement JWT-based auth
- Add login/logout endpoints
- Protect dashboard route

## Test plan
- [ ] Login with valid credentials succeeds
- [ ] Login with invalid credentials returns 401
- [ ] Protected route redirects unauthenticated users
- [ ] JWT refresh works after expiry
EOF
)" \
  --reviewer @org/team-name \
  --label "feature,needs-review"
```

### Issue triage
```bash
# List open issues by label
gh issue list --label "bug" --state open

# Create issue from template
gh issue create --title "Bug: login fails on Safari" \
  --body "Steps to reproduce..." \
  --label "bug" --assignee @me

# Close issue and link PR
gh issue close 42 --comment "Fixed in #57"
```

### PR review workflow
```bash
# Check PR status
gh pr status

# View PR diff
gh pr diff 57

# Approve and merge
gh pr review 57 --approve --body "LGTM"
gh pr merge 57 --squash --delete-branch
```

### Release management
```bash
# Create release from tag
gh release create v1.2.0 \
  --title "v1.2.0 — Performance improvements" \
  --notes "$(git log --oneline v1.1.0..v1.2.0)" \
  --target main
```

### Checklist
- [ ] PR title follows conventional commits format
- [ ] PR description has summary + test plan
- [ ] Labels applied (feature/bug/chore)
- [ ] Reviewer assigned
- [ ] Issue linked in PR body (closes #N)
- [ ] Branch deleted after merge

name: Git Worktrees — Isolated Parallel Dev Branches
description: Use git worktrees to work on multiple branches simultaneously without stashing or switching. Each worktree is a separate working directory sharing the same repo.
template:
## Git Worktrees

### Core Concept
Worktrees = multiple working directories → one repo.
No stash, no branch switching, no lost context.

### Setup
```bash
# List existing worktrees
git worktree list

# Add worktree for a new feature branch
git worktree add ../feature-auth feature/auth
# → creates /project-feature-auth/ checked out on feature/auth

# Add worktree for existing branch
git worktree add ../hotfix-login hotfix/login-crash

# Add worktree with a new branch
git worktree add -b feature/payments ../payments-wt origin/main
```

### Workflow
```bash
# Main feature work
cd /project
git checkout feature/dashboard

# Meanwhile, urgent hotfix in parallel
cd /project-hotfix
# full working tree — run tests, edit files, commit independently
git commit -am "fix: prevent null pointer in login"
git push origin hotfix/login-crash

# Back to feature — zero context loss
cd /project
```

### Cleanup
```bash
# Remove worktree (safe — doesn't delete branch)
git worktree remove ../project-hotfix

# Force remove if dirty
git worktree remove --force ../project-hotfix

# Prune stale worktree metadata
git worktree prune
```

### When to use
- Parallel feature branches without constant branch switching
- Hotfix while mid-feature
- Reviewing a PR while keeping local changes intact
- Running tests on multiple branches simultaneously

### Limitations
- Cannot check out the same branch in two worktrees
- `node_modules/` not shared — run `npm install` in each worktree
- IDE may not support multiple worktrees in one window

### Checklist
- [ ] Worktrees in sibling directories (not nested in repo)
- [ ] `git worktree prune` run periodically
- [ ] Dependencies installed in each worktree independently
- [ ] Worktrees removed when branch merged
